items.text: lume review ingest writes review folders under .lume/review/<date>-NN/ (e.g. .lume/review/2026-06-11-01/), not at the .lume root: FilesystemStore review-artifact path, the next-free daily-sequence scan, and the findings.md writer all use the new location; SQLite backing behaviour unchanged
The existing .lume/review-2026-06-11-01/ folder is migrated to .lume/review/2026-06-11-01/ (findings.md + result.json intact) and no review-* folder remains at the .lume root
Tests assert the new path: at least one test references .lume/review/ sequencing/location and the full suite is green with no assertion weakened
All docs naming the convention (plugin/skills/lume/SKILL.md, plugin/README.md, root README.md) say .lume/review/<date>-NN and no doc still says .lume/review-<date>

The previously orphaned state is landed: gap records under .lume/gaps/ (G1, G2), the migrated review folder, and this workstream's own state are committed - nothing under .lume/ is left uncommitted at handback
G1 is marked resolved (lume gap resolve lume G1) with a resolution pointing at this workstream; G2's general design question stays open (only its immediate instance is landed here)

dod.preamble: Close gaps G1 and (the immediate instance of) G2 in one execution iteration: relocate review output to .lume/review/<date>-NN/, migrate the existing folder, update docs and tests, and commit all currently-orphaned .lume state through this workstream.
